title: top things I LOVED & HATED about Auburn University from an Alumni - *SUPER HONEST*
body: Auburn had atrocious parking. I liked being a student there but good lord the parking for Auburn is insane! I only had C, which was fine  for my first two years but for my last few years I did not pay for parking because I was able to get an apartment closer to school that had the transit nearby

title: top things I LOVED & HATED about Auburn University from an Alumni - *SUPER HONEST*
body: Hi! Minor here, I’d love to be a vet, I live in Florida at the moment (about 4-5 hours from auburn) I’ve talked to my parents about what they would think if I end up going there, I’ve heard really awesome things, my family and I have been going to games for a while now, we always have a blast, the sorority’s are beautiful, I would love to be in one but not sure, all the clubs sound amazing, not sure where I’ll be in life by the time I’m in college, I hope I still wanna be a vet, (yes I know they have other programs and stuff to, but they are one of the best veterinary schools, so-)
author: @Bee-r7j9p

title: top things I LOVED & HATED about Auburn University from an Alumni - *SUPER HONEST*
body:
Been out many years but go back often. Interesting video. Auburn has ridiculous growth. Best thing about Auburn besides getting a good education is its a college town and a real family. I still talk to and see many of my friends from Auburn today or at games.
Worst thing parking is an absolute racket.
Also A lot of schools are in big cities like Georgia Tech and USC are commuter schools no real campus life unlike AU
War 🦅
author: @AUgrad2000

title: top things I LOVED & HATED about Auburn University from an Alumni - *SUPER HONEST*

title: top things I LOVED & HATED about Auburn University from an Alumni - *SUPER HONEST*
body: You are not an “alumni” you are an alumna.  Alumni refers to a group of men or a mixed group of men and women.  One woman is an alumna.  Two or more women are alumnae.  Regards, an Auburn alumnus.

title: MORNING IN MY LIFE AT THE UNIVERSITY OF ALABAMA | friday morning vlog
body: For everyone saying she is a kid of a rich family. She runs all of her social media platforms alone which make you gain a lot of money. Her BMW is a hand me down from her mom from 2010. Most of her skincare is PR. She does really nice videos about all of the unboxing and that is where most of her skincare and make up is from. In her Amazon storefront you can see some of the things that she used to decorate her room and they do not cost an insane amount of money. I think we should be happy for her and also appreciate the things that we have in life, without looking so much into others people life and choosing either to say if the person grew up rich or poor.

title: UNIVERSITY OF ALABAMA *CAMPUS TOUR* | Classrooms, Sorority Row, Stadium, Starbucks, Library, Dorms
body: Great video tour! You sold this family on UA! It looks like someone put a lot of TLC into that campus. Very beautiful. The little study booths were a highlight. Such an inspiring space to study. Sorority row was impressive. I guess I didn’t expect much thinking they would be  run of the mill Greek houses(big, but a little tired, needs help). I didn’t expect to see 20 something immaculate Barbie dream houses mega sized. This tour changed my daughter’s options from UA being an option, to UA being the priority.

author: @user-y1o7o

title: UNIVERSITY OF ALABAMA *CAMPUS TOUR* | Classrooms, Sorority Row, Stadium, Starbucks, Library, Dorms
body:
Many things changed, since I was a student at UoA (1977-1984), the ΔΔΔ house was across the street from where it is now and it was round.
Barnwell field, that we used to play soccer is now a parking lot.
But the ΔΓ anchor, even with it's new brass color (it was dark colored in my days) reminded me of a time that our ΘΤ (back then it was a men's fraternity and ΔΓs were our Little Sisters, I think this changed now because in the new form ΘΤ became coed because is was a professional engineering fraternity and I believe the old scheme was dissolved for a while) pledges hand cuffed me on the anchor and as our members were chasing them around, in order to get the handcuff's key to free me, they lost the key in the grass lol.
I got so mad with the whole thing, that I put the double side of the cuff my hand was tied with, over a blade near the top of the ΔΓ anchor and using my arm as a lever, I broke the pin of the hand cuffs and set my self free, with only minor cuts on the sides of my wrist.
So the ΔΓ anchor has it's history with me too lol.
author: @FLORATOSOTHON
